summary: Large light-gun-related item/fighter decompilation slice. The PR added C implementations for itlgunbeam.c, itlgunray.c, and ft_0CDD.c, introduced light gun beam/ray item vars and attrs, tightened several prototypes from UNK/s32 to Vec3 or HSD_GObj forms, and adjusted related fire flower/light gun/fox blaster call sites. The retained and reformatted itlgunbeam.s/itlgunray.s assembly suggests this was work-in-progress or partial/nonmatching decomp support rather than a clean asm deletion. There were no PR body notes or review comments, so intent beyond the title and diff is weakly evidenced.
what_changed: Added a 691-line ft_0CDD.c implementation covering common fighter item shoot logic for LGunShoot, LGunShootAir, FireFlowerShoot, and FireFlowerShootAir, plus helper routines around item-held shooting, motion transitions, effects, and callbacks.

Added itlgunbeam.c with light gun beam spawn/update/collision/reflection style routines including it_802993E0, it_80299528, it_802996D0, it_802998A0, it_8029999C, it_802999E4, it_80299A68, and small callback stubs.

Added itlgunray.c with light gun ray spawn/update/collision/reflection style routines including it_80298168, it_802982AC, it_80298300, it_802985D8, it_802985F8, it_80298654, it_80298828, and it_802988C4.
Extended itCommonItems.h with ItLGunBeamAttr, ItLGunBeamVars, ItLGunRayAttr, and ItLGunRayVars, and added lgunbeam/lgunray variants to Item.xDD4_itemVar in src/melee/it/types.h.
Retyped ftCommon motion variables for item throw/shoot state from UNK_T to concrete float/int fields in ftCommon/types.h.
Updated ft_0CDD.h to replace several UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations with concrete void functions taking Fighter_GObj*, Fighter*, and Vec3*.
Changed light gun helper signatures: it_8028E774 now takes Vec3* and writes x/y/z, and it_8028E79C now takes an f32 direction argument.
Changed fire flower helper it_80292F14 from taking an s32 argument to taking Vec3*, matching its position-vector use from ft_0CDD.c.
Changed itlgunbeam.h and itlgunray.h reference callback prototypes from Item_GObj* reference parameters to HSD_GObj* where used by it_8026B894-style APIs.
Reformatted/regenerated itlgunbeam.s and itlgunray.s with .file, .text, .fn/.endfn, .obj/.endobj, decimal bit indices, and operand formatting changes while retaining assembly content.
Made small baselib state initializer cleanup by replacing empty aggregate initializers like {} with explicit { 0 } for color structs.
smart_moves: Converting light gun/fire flower helper prototypes to Vec3* made call sites communicate actual position-vector semantics instead of opaque integers or placeholder attributes.

Adding item-specific lgunbeam and lgunray vars under Item.xDD4_itemVar gave the new C code named storage for offsets like xDD4, xDE0, xDF8, and xE04 instead of scattering raw offsets throughout the item logic.
The C code preserves important math constants and angle-normalization loops around -pi/pi and 0/2pi, which are central to matching projectile reflection and aiming behavior.

The beam spawn path uses the existing SpawnItem pattern: fill kind, prev_pos/pos, facing_dir, parent gobj, damage, velocity, x44 flag, and x40 before Item_80268B18, which is reusable for future projectile item decompilation.
State tables were added beside the new item C files, preserving the project convention of ItemStateTable arrays pointing at anim/phys/coll callback functions.

The assembly files were normalized with function/object metadata rather than left in older raw label form, improving searchability and address-to-function mapping while the C work remained incomplete or nonmatching.
decomp_lessons: Light gun beam/ray behavior depends heavily on correctly modeling Item.xDD4_itemVar payloads; future work should refine ItLGunBeamVars and ItLGunRayVars rather than reverting to anonymous offset access.
Projectile spawn functions should usually accept a Vec3* position plus owner HSD_GObj/Item_GObj and facing direction; this PR shows several older placeholder prototypes were misleading.

When a fighter action drives an item shot, decompilation often requires touching both fighter motion-state code and item projectile code in the same slice because callbacks, effects, and item helper signatures cross subsystem boundaries.

The diff contains many comments such as 'Not sure if this struct size is correct or type of vars' and 'Not sure if this is the right bit'; these are useful markers for future verification rather than settled documentation.
Angle normalization loops using M_PI, 2*M_PI, and M_PI_2 are match-sensitive and should be preserved structurally until the exact compiler output is understood.
Header cleanup can unlock broader decomp progress: changing it_80292F14, it_8028E774, it_8028E79C, it_802996D0, it_80299C28, and it_802988C4 prototypes clarified several downstream call sites.
naming_conventions: New item attribute structs use the pattern ItLGunBeamAttr and ItLGunRayAttr.
New item variable structs use the pattern ItLGunBeamVars and ItLGunRayVars.
Item union members were named lowercase by item kind: lgunbeam and lgunray.
Unmatched or not-yet-renamed functions kept address-based names such as it_802996D0, it_80298168, ftCo_800CE650, and ft_800CE528.
Common fighter action callbacks follow ftCo_<Action>_<Phase>, e.g. ftCo_LGunShoot_Anim, ftCo_LGunShootAir_Phys, ftCo_FireFlowerShoot_Coll.
Static data retained address-style names such as it_804DCC20, it_803F6630, it_804DCBD0, and it_803F64F8.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Retained asm/melee/it/items/itlgunbeam.s and asm/melee/it/items/itlgunray.s while adding C files, suggesting the C was not a complete asm-removal match in this slice.
Assembly was converted to the newer annotation style with .file, .text, .balign 4, .fn/.endfn, and .obj/.endobj directives.
Diff shows operand-format normalization such as stw r0, 4(r1) to stw r0, 0x4(r1), clrlwi bit counts to decimal, and or. r29,r3,r3 to mr. r29,r3.
C implementations used explicit goto loop labels for angle wrapping, likely to preserve original control flow shape around repeated add/subtract of 2*pi.
Math constants were emitted as static floats/doubles near the item C files to correspond to small-data constants referenced from assembly.
Several functions intentionally kept uncertain comments and disabled alternatives with #if 0, which is evidence that matching/typing was still exploratory.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the PR slice.
No explicit reviewer rationale or requested changes were available; conclusions are based only on title, changed files, and diff content.
follow_up_queries: Check later PRs touching itlgunbeam.c or itlgunray.c to see whether these exploratory structs and comments were refined into matching code.
Search for ItLGunBeamVars and ItLGunRayVars to confirm final field names, types, and struct sizes.
Search for it_802996D0 and it_80298168 call sites to verify final spawn-function signatures and owner_gobj typing.
Review later changes to ft_0CDD.c for cleanup of p_ftCommonData offset casts and uncertain fighter motion variable fields.
Search for PRs removing asm/melee/it/items/itlgunbeam.s or asm/melee/it/items/itlgunray.s to identify when the light gun projectile code actually matched.
